patches.summary: auctions-aftermarket (POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/aftermarket/listings/bids) now requires a JSON body piped via --stdin instead of hardcoding an empty body; without --stdin it errors (exit 2), and an empty/invalid body fails to parse rather than being sent.
patches.reason: Greptile P-level: body was hardcoded `map[string]any{}` and POSTed verbatim with no body input, so the bid command always sent an empty {} body (silent no-op). The endpoint places multiple bids and has no per-field body flags, so it mirrors the require-stdin array-body pattern from domains records replace.
patches.validated_outcome: No --stdin exits 2 with '--stdin is required'; empty --stdin fails JSON parse (no {} sent); a valid `[{...}]` body via --stdin --dry-run shows the array body in the request preview.

patches.summary: MCP binary-response handler passes the client's binaryResponseEnvelope JSON through verbatim (detected via the _pp_binary discriminator) instead of base64-encoding it a second time; removed the now-unused encoding/base64 import.
patches.reason: The HTTP client already wraps genuinely binary bodies into a self-describing {"_pp_binary":true,"encoding":"base64","data":...} envelope. makeAPIHandler then base64-encoded that whole JSON string AGAIN, double-encoding the payload and discarding the envelope metadata. When binaryResponse is set but the API returns JSON/text, the body is not enveloped, so the handler must detect the envelope rather than blindly re-encode.

patches.summary: agent-context archetype corrected from "payments" to "developer-platform".
patches.reason: "payments" was a copy-paste artifact; GoDaddy is a domain registrar / developer platform (developer-tools category). "developer-platform" matches the sibling apify CLI in the same category.

patches.summary: Re-assert owner-only mode on cache/config/store/state paths via os.Chmod after MkdirAll/WriteFile/OpenFile, since those calls only set mode at creation time.
patches.reason: Security: os.WriteFile/os.MkdirAll/O_CREATE do not change the mode of an existing file or dir. Files/dirs left from an older 0o644/0o755 build therefore stayed world-readable despite the perms tightening. Added best-effort os.Chmod after each sensitive write/mkdir in cache.go, config.go, client.go, store.go (db dir), profile.go and feedback.go (state dir + appended ledger). store.go already chmod'd the SQLite file + WAL/SHM sidecars; this adds the missing db-dir chmod.

patches.summary: Risk annotation classifier uses the exact POST-path allowlist (isUngatedValidationRoute) that mirrors client.go's isValidationStyleMutation, instead of a broad "validate" substring heuristic.
patches.reason: Greptile P1: the substring heuristic tagged certificates validate and agents verify-acme as Barrier:none, but client.go's exact allowlist still gated them, so the help text contradicted runtime enforcement (ErrWriteRequiresApproval). The two functions now share the same allowlist and stay in sync.

patches.summary: 403 error hint tells users GoDaddy requires GODADDY_API_KEY/GODADDY_API_SECRET and to verify key scope/account eligibility.
patches.reason: Greptile P1: the old hint claimed "This API is configured without credentials", which is wrong for GoDaddy (credentials are required) and misled users into ignoring missing or under-scoped keys.

patches.summary: Cache dir/file permissions tightened to 0o700/0o600 from 0o755/0o644.
patches.reason: Greptile P1/security: world-readable cache files leaked GoDaddy API responses (domain ownership, shopper, certificate data) to other local users on a shared machine.

patches.summary: SQLite store dir/file (and WAL/SHM sidecars) restricted to owner-only (0o700/0o600).
patches.reason: Greptile P1/security: OpenWithContext created the db dir 0o755 and the driver created the db file with umask defaults, leaving cached GoDaddy API data world-readable on a shared machine.

patches.summary: shoppers factors change-password validates --secret is present in the non-stdin path.
patches.reason: Greptile P1: the empty '!stdinBody' guard let the command send an empty body, producing a cryptic API 400 instead of a clear CLI error.

patches.summary: DNS record add/replace commands require --stdin (their only body input).
patches.reason: Greptile P1: empty !stdinBody guards let these commands send an empty body, producing a cryptic API 400 instead of a clear CLI error.

patches.summary: store.List treats negative limit as unbounded; analytics/data_source pass -1 for the full dataset.
patches.reason: db.List(...,0) clamped to 200, silently truncating analytics/full-read callers to 200 rows.

patches.summary: Require --stdin on 20 additional stdin-only mutation commands (customer domains, agents verify, certificates email/cancel, etc.).
patches.reason: Greptile P1 pattern (flagged on DNS records + aftermarket previously): empty '!stdinBody' guards on stdin-only commands silently sent {} to the API, producing cryptic 400s. Same usageErr require-stdin fix applied to the remaining stdin-only mutation commands.

patches.summary: --deliver file:<path> creates intermediate dirs with 0o700 instead of 0o755.
patches.reason: Greptile: a world-listable deliver dir let other local users enumerate delivered output filenames (which can reveal domain names) even though file contents are 0o600. Made consistent with cache.go/store.go.

patches.summary: MCP read-only SQL tool surfaces rows.Columns/rows.Scan/rows.Err errors instead of silently discarding them.
patches.reason: Greptile: discarded Scan errors returned rows with partial zero-value data and no warning; a Columns error yielded nil columns and silently no-op scans for every row.

patches.summary: Transport-level request failures back off (2^attempt seconds) before retry, matching the 5xx path; added regression tests.
patches.reason: Greptile: network errors (connection refused, DNS failure, timeout) previously retried with only the rate-limiter delay, producing up to four rapid-fire retries that hammer a recovering endpoint.

patches.summary: agent-context now reports auth mode "api-key" and the GODADDY_API_KEY/GODADDY_API_SECRET/GODADDY_AUTH_HEADER env vars instead of "none" with an empty list.
patches.reason: Greptile: hardcoded authMode "none" and empty envVars meant agents bootstrapping from agent-context output could not discover the required GoDaddy credentials.

patches.summary: Seven mutation commands with all-optional body fields whose defaults are empty/zero now require at least one body flag (or --stdin) instead of silently sending an empty {} body.
patches.reason: Repo-wide sweep of the change-password empty-'!stdinBody' pattern: these commands had no-op empty guards, so running them with no flags and no --stdin sent {} to the API and produced a cryptic 400. Commands whose flag defaults already populate the body (e.g. certificates renew/reissue with non-empty common-name/csr/root-type defaults) were left unchanged because they never emit an empty body.

patches.summary: domains available-bulk (POST /v1/domains/available) and dnssecRecords PATCH now unmarshal stdin into []any and send a JSON array, matching GoDaddy's array request-body contract instead of forcing map[string]any.
patches.reason: Repo-wide body-shape sweep (same class as the earlier DNS records []DNSRecord fix): these two endpoints take JSON arrays per GoDaddy's API, but the generated code forced map[string]any, so piping the correct array body failed with 'cannot unmarshal array into Go value of type map'. The commands were effectively unusable for their real contract.

patches.summary: 401 error hint now names GODADDY_API_KEY/GODADDY_API_SECRET and notes production/OTE keys are not interchangeable, matching the corrected 403 hint.
patches.reason: Auth-hint sweep (follow-on to the 403 fix): the generic '401: check your API credentials' hint did not tell a user with unset/wrong credentials which env vars to set, contradicting the specificity of the 403 hint.

patches.summary: MCP server 401/403 error hints corrected to name GODADDY_API_KEY/GODADDY_API_SECRET; removed the misleading 'this API is configured without credentials' 403 text.
patches.reason: Auth-hint sweep: the MCP tool error path carried the same 'configured without credentials' bug already fixed in the CLI's helpers.go 403 hint, plus a generic 401 hint. GoDaddy requires credentials, so the old MCP hints misled agents into ignoring missing/under-scoped keys.

patches.summary: Corrected the isSyncAccessWarning doc comment that wrongly claimed 'this CLI has no auth'.
patches.reason: Auth-hint sweep: the generated stub comment misrepresented GoDaddy's auth model (the CLI requires GODADDY_API_KEY/GODADDY_API_SECRET). Behavior is unchanged; only the misleading comment was fixed.

patches.summary: --password, --secret, and --auth-code flag help text now warns that passing the value on argv exposes it in shell history/process listings and points to --stdin as the safe path.
patches.reason: Secret-as-CLI-flag sweep: these flags carry credentials/transfer auth codes through argv (visible in 'ps aux' and shell history). The flags are part of the generated contract and verify-skill gate so they cannot be removed; the secure --stdin path now is documented at point-of-use.
